baseline_guidance: Run each eval with this skill and compare against a no-skill baseline or the previous skill version; pass only when the skilled run better satisfies the assertions without losing required safety or precision.

evals.prompt: Take over this repository with a Codex dev team and keep going until every agent-actionable open issue is handled.
evals.expected_output: A coordinated long-running issue-to-merge loop that loads the coordinator and status protocol skills, builds a dependency graph, assigns role agents in parallel-safe worktrees, shepherds PRs through checks and review, compacts status, runs the closure gate, and stops only for merged issues or true external blockers.
assertions: Loads and follows team-coordinator and agent-team-status-protocol before coordinating work.
Prioritizes open PRs, failing checks, review threads, and repo-local blockers ahead of downstream issue work.
Starts every independent ready issue or fix stream when local resources allow.
Requires checker evidence and maintainer-style review before merge readiness.

Requires every PR body to include full GitHub-visible context: linked issue, related issues/PRs, stacked/base/downstream references, scope, validation, skipped checks, risks, and merge or retargeting notes.
Treats missing PR context as blocking until the PR body is updated.
Treats the maintainer-reviewer agent's Approve decision as review acceptance even when GitHub cannot record a same-author formal approval.
Does not wait for or request a non-author formal GitHub approver unless an actual merge attempt is rejected by branch protection or permissions.
Does not mark dependency-blocked downstream issues complete while upstream agent-actionable work remains.
Records owners, branches, worktrees, PR URLs, gates, blockers, cycle counts, and next actions in STATUS.md.
Before completion, verifies open issues/PRs, worktree state, repository cleanliness, status compaction, and retrospective recommendation disposition.

evals.prompt: A builder finished and the PR merged. Clean up its local Git worktree as part of the dev-team loop.
evals.expected_output: A cleanup decision that validates the exact worktree path and branch before removal, confirms pushed/recorded/clean state, preserves worktrees needed for follow-up, and asks for explicit user approval before discarding dirty, unpushed, or unrecorded state.
assertions: Runs or requires git worktree list --porcelain before choosing the worktree to remove.
Runs or requires git status --short --branch inside the candidate worktree.
Checks that the branch is pushed and the PR or commit is recorded in STATUS.md.
Preserves the worktree when review or CI follow-up is likely.
Does not force-remove, reset, clean, or discard dirty state without explicit approval for the exact path and branch.

evals.prompt: All target PRs merged and issues are closed. Finish the dev-team goal.
evals.expected_output: Runs a closure gate that reconciles GitHub issues/PRs, local worktrees, repository cleanliness, hot/cold status files, and retrospective recommendation dispositions before declaring completion.
assertions: Checks gh issue list and gh pr list for remaining target work.
Reconciles git worktree list --porcelain with STATUS.md.
Reconciles git status --short --branch --untracked-files=all with STATUS.md cleanliness notes.
Moves historical contracts, handoffs, checker logs, old gates, review bodies, and completed retrospectives to STATUS.archive.md.
Requires every create-process-issue or assign-builder recommendation to have a tracker URL, assignment, completed PR, or explicit declined/downgraded reason.
